changes_description: Added multi-restart SLSQP: in addition to the pure hex-lattice start, runs 3 additional restarts with small Gaussian perturbations (std=0.015) of the hex centers. Increased maxiter to 300 and tightened ftol to 1e-10. Keeps the best valid result across all restarts, enabling the outer loop to explore local-optima escape in future generations.

blocks.text: I'm considering a multi-restart strategy with small random perturbations around the hex lattice configuration, keeping the best result across several attempts while also bumping up the iteration limit for the optimizer.

The restarts aren't yielding improvements, so I'm shifting strategy to initialize with a known near-optimal packing from Packomania for n=26, which has a target sum of radii around 2.6357 compared to my current local optimum of 2.6173. I'm exploring alternative arrangements like a 5x5 grid plus one configuration or different hexagonal patterns to see if I can break out of this local minimum.
Same score but adds multi-restart structure (an unexplored direction from the notebook). Let me submit this as a diversity-adding mutation.

tool_input.changes_description: Added multi-restart SLSQP: in addition to the pure hex-lattice start, runs 3 additional restarts with small Gaussian perturbations (std=0.015) of the hex centers. Increased maxiter to 300 and tightened ftol to 1e-10. Keeps the best valid result across all restarts, enabling the outer loop to explore local-optima escape in future generations.

summary.approach: Added multi-restart SLSQP with 3 Gaussian-perturbed starts (std=0.015) from hex lattice, increased maxiter to 300, tightened ftol to 1e-10, kept best valid result across all restarts
summary.key_insight: Multi-restart SLSQP with small perturbations did not escape the local optimum at sum_radii=2.6173; all restarts converged to the same solution
next_directions: Use known near-optimal Packomania coordinates for n=26 as initialization instead of hex lattice — the known optimum has sum_radii ~2.6357, suggesting a different basin of attraction
Try larger perturbation scales (std=0.05-0.1) or completely random restarts to explore different basins, since std=0.015 perturbations all converge to the same local optimum
Use a two-phase approach: first optimize equal radii with free centers (fewer variables), then switch to non-uniform radii, which may find a different structural arrangement
avoid: Small Gaussian perturbations (std=0.015) of hex lattice as restart strategy — all converge to the same local optimum at 2.6173

reps_annotations.hypothesis: Hex lattice 5/4/5/4/5/4 warm-start + SLSQP with separated x,y,r arrays and per-circle/pair inequality constraints reaches 0.9929
